 {"id":"ft-3kxe.5","title":"Operational telemetry pipeline — structured tracing, histograms, resource tracking","description":"# Operational Telemetry Pipeline — Structured Tracing, Histograms, Resource Tracking

## Skills: /extreme-software-optimization

## Goal
Build a unified telemetry pipeline that collects structured metrics, traces, and resource observations from all FrankenTerm subsystems.

## What it collects
1. **Structured tracing**: spans for every operation (capture, process, store) with timing and context
2. **Histograms**: latency distributions for key operations (capture, PDU encode/decode, storage write)
3. **Resource tracking**:
   - RSS, virtual memory, FD count for mux server process
   - Per-pane CPU and memory (via process tree)
   - Disk I/O rates (SQLite write throughput)
   - Network stats (mux connection health)

## Platform-specific resource collection
- **Linux**: /proc/<pid>/status (VmRSS, VmSize), /proc/<pid>/fd/ (FD count), /proc/<pid>/io (read/write bytes)
- **macOS**: proc_pidinfo(PROC_PIDTASKINFO) for RSS/CPU, proc_pidinfo(PROC_PIDLISTFDS) for FD count, no direct /proc/*/io equivalent — use getrusage() for I/O approximation
- Abstract behind a `SystemMetrics` trait with platform-specific impls

## Storage
- In-memory circular buffer for recent metrics (last 1 hour, 30s resolution)
- SQLite table for long-term storage (hourly aggregates)
- Prometheus-compatible exposition endpoint (optional feature)

## Integration points
- Survival model (wa-1qz1.1): consumes RSS observations
- Conformal prediction (wa-1qz1.4): consumes resource time series
- Auto-tuning (wa-ssm4): uses metric distributions
- MuxWatchdog (wa-2cha): consumes health metrics
- Memory pressure engine (wa-2ahu0): consumes RSS per-pane

## Tests
- **Criterion benchmarks**: metric recording overhead <100ns per metric point
- Verify structured tracing spans nest correctly
- Verify histogram quantile accuracy (p50, p95, p99 within 5%)
- **Test both Linux and macOS metric collection paths**
- Verify circular buffer eviction at capacity
- Integration: metrics flow from collection to storage to API

## Acceptance criteria
- Unified metric collection across all subsystems
- Platform-specific resource collection (Linux + macOS) behind SystemMetrics trait
- <100ns per metric recording (no hot-path overhead)
- Histogram quantiles accurate to within 5%
- Optional Prometheus exposition endpoint
- In-memory + SQLite storage with configurable retention","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:24:19.006449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:08:24.549615Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:08:24.549615Z","close_reason":"SystemMetrics trait + PlatformMetrics impl + collect_system_memory() + TelemetryStore (SQLite hourly aggregates with WAL, upsert, time-range queries, retention pruning) + 11 new tests (44 total passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3kxe.6","title":"Fork Hardening benchmark and test suite (ESO methodology)","description":"## Goal
Comprehensive benchmark and test suite for Fork Hardening changes, following the ESO methodology: golden outputs, isomorphism proofs, before/after measurements.

## Test Categories

### 1. Memory Leak Regression Tests
- Start mux with 50 simulated panes
- Run for 1 hour with continuous output
- Measure RSS at start, every 5 minutes, and at end
- PASS criteria: RSS growth < 1MB/hour (down from ~130MB/hour)
- Golden outputs: RSS curve must be within 10% of baseline

### 2. Lock-Free Buffer Tests
- Concurrent push/pop correctness (no data loss, no corruption)
- Overflow behavior (gap emission after threshold)
- Performance: P99 capture latency < 5ms under 50 panes

### 3. Differential Snapshot Tests
- Dirty tracking accuracy (only changed panes serialized)
- Diff chain application (base + N diffs = correct state)
- Continuous saving loop stability (24-hour stress test)
- Sub-100ms save time for typical diffs

### 4. cgroups Integration Tests (Linux only)
- Memory limit enforcement (mux stays within budget)
- Proactive save trigger at threshold
- Alert generation at critical threshold
- Fallback behavior on macOS (monitoring-only)

### 5. Telemetry Accuracy Tests
- Histogram accuracy vs known latency distribution
- Resource tracking accuracy vs /proc values
- Span hierarchy correctness (parent-child relationships)

### 6. Isomorphism Proofs (per patch)
Each mux server patch includes:
```
## Change: [description]
- Ordering preserved:     [yes/no + why]
- Tie-breaking unchanged: [yes/no + why]
- Floating-point:         [identical/N/A]
- Golden outputs:         sha256sum -c golden_checksums.txt ✓
```

### Implementation Location
- crates/wa-core/tests/fork_hardening_tests.rs
- crates/wa-core/benches/fork_hardening_benchmarks.rs
- scripts/profiling/ (heaptrack scripts, RSS monitoring)

## Dependencies
- Depends on all Fork Hardening beads being implemented
- Memory leak tests require Linux machine (trj or css)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Memory leak regression test passing (< 1MB/hour growth)
- Lock-free buffer correctness and performance tests
- Differential snapshot accuracy and speed benchmarks
- cgroups integration tests (Linux) and fallback tests (macOS)
- Telemetry accuracy validation
- All ESO isomorphism proofs documented per patch
- CI integration for regression detection

## Test Framework Requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks**: Comprehensive benchmarks for ALL hardening improvements:
  - Memory leak regression: benchmark RSS growth rate per 1000 capture cycles (target <1KB/cycle)
  - Lock-free SPSC buffer: benchmark push/pop throughput under contention (target >10M ops/sec)
  - Differential snapshot: benchmark diff computation time for varying pane counts (10, 50, 200) and dirty ratios (1%, 10%, 50%)
  - Snapshot save latency: benchmark full save cycle (dirty detection + diff + serialize + write). Target <100ms for typical diffs.
  - Telemetry overhead: benchmark the cost of tracing instrumentation. Target <1% overhead vs uninstrumented code.
  - Before/after comparison: each benchmark must record a baseline (before hardening) and improved (after hardening) measurement for the ESO isomorphism proof.
- **Proptest for regression detection**: Property-based tests that catch regressions:
  - Generate random pane output sequences, apply all hardening patches, and verify output is bit-identical to unhardened path (behavioral isomorphism)
  - Generate random capture schedules (varying timing, ordering), verify all pane data is captured correctly regardless of schedule
  - Generate random memory pressure scenarios, verify eviction decisions are deterministic and reproducible
  - For each ESO patch: generate random inputs, verify the golden output checksum matches (automated isomorphism verification)

## Cross-References
- **wa-3cyp.1** (Performance benchmark suite): The fork hardening benchmarks are a subset of the overall performance benchmark suite. Share benchmark infrastructure (MockMuxServer, test helpers, criterion configuration). The fork hardening benchmarks should be runnable both standalone and as part of the full benchmark suite.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deliver comprehensive hardening validation suite (benchmarks, regression tests, soak/failure tests) aligned with ESO proof standards.
2. Ensure benchmark harnesses and golden outputs detect regressions in memory, latency, throughput, and reliability under swarm-scale workloads.
3. Require comprehensive validation matrix: unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end scripts, long-run soak, and adversarial/failure-injection scenarios.
4. Require structured diagnostics/logging and machine-readable artifacts for every benchmark/test phase, including reproducibility metadata and diff-friendly summaries.
5. Closure requires evidence that hardening gates pass against explicit thresholds and that unresolved risks are documented with owners.","notes":"Claimed after bv robot triage surfaced wa-3kxe as top start-work item. Starting wa-3kxe.6 benchmark/test-suite scaffolding now, focusing on lock-free buffer, diff snapshot, telemetry, and regression harness pieces that do not overlap wa-3kxe.1 memory-leak patch implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:24:19.892536Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-28T00:06:35.726528Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T00:06:35.726428Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":764,"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.6","author":"CalmOwl","text":"Starting wa-3kxe.6 now.  {"id":"ft-3kxe.7","title":"[Subtask wa-3kxe.6] Add fork-hardening benchmark harness slices (ring buffer, diff snapshot, telemetry)","description":"Implement an unblocked benchmark slice under wa-3kxe that adds reusable Criterion coverage for lock-free capture path, differential snapshot capture path, and telemetry instrumentation overhead.

Acceptance criteria:
- New benchmark suite compiles and runs under cargo bench -p frankenterm-core.
- Bench groups include explicit budget metadata emitted via bench_common.
- Coverage includes ring-buffer throughput, diff-snapshot dirty-capture path, and telemetry overhead baseline vs instrumented path.
- Add/adjust CI budget prefix mapping so the new benchmark groups are enforceable in scripts/check_bench_budgets.sh.
- Add bead comments summarizing coordination and scope boundaries with wa-3kxe.1 / wa-3kxe.6 owners.","notes":"Implemented fork-hardening benchmark harness slice:
- Added new Criterion bench target: crates/frankenterm-core/benches/fork_hardening.rs
- Added cargo bench registration in crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml
- Added CI budget prefixes in crates/frankenterm-core/benches/bench_common.rs and scripts/check_bench_budgets.sh

Validation:
- cargo fmt --check ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-calmowl cargo check --all-targets ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-calmowl cargo check -p frankenterm-core --bench fork_hardening ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-calmowl cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --bench fork_hardening -- -D warnings ✅
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-calmowl cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench fork_hardening -- --warm-up-time 0.1 --measurement-time 0.1 --sample-size 10 ✅

Note: full workspace clippy --all-targets currently fails on existing concurrent untracked test file crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fork_hardening_tests.rs (float-cmp lint), outside this slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmOwl","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:59:21.978282Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:11:55.546641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:11:55.546627Z","close_reason":"Completed benchmark harness slice: new fork_hardening Criterion bench + budget prefix wiring + targeted validation and bench execution.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","performance","testing","track-hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3kxe.7","depends_on_id":"ft-3kxe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-c5dn","title":"Implement write batching + WAL checkpoint/optimize strategy","description":"# Task: Write batching + checkpoint/optimize strategy

## Goal
Reduce write amplification and keep WAL/FTS healthy under sustained ingest.

## Requirements
- Writer batching:
  - batch segment inserts and index updates
  - bounded batch size (time/rows/bytes)
- WAL strategy:
  - periodic checkpoints based on size/time
  - avoid long stalls (do work incrementally)
- FTS maintenance:
  - decide when to run optimize/vacuum-like operations
  - ensure maintenance is explicit and does not surprise users

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - simulate sustained ingest and assert:
    - WAL does not grow without bound
    - queries remain available
- Benchmarks:
  - compare before/after ingest throughput

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sustained ingest remains stable and storage maintenance is predictable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:41:21.359421809Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.236193Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T04:11:44.059438251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
